data.epi_content:
Vivian was standing in front of the classroom door, looking around cautiously.

"Were you waiting for me?"

She shakes her head.

Then, as if noticing something strange on my face, she comes closer and gently touches the cheek that Lydia had just slapped.

I hate being touched.

I guess she doesn't even care about that anymore.

"...Miss, your cheek is slightly red. What happened?"

"I fell asleep face down and it got pressed."

"Oh, that's a relief."

Is that a convincing excuse?

Well, all I ever did in Vivian's room was read novels and fall asleep face down anyway.

Thanks to that, Lydia and her group have been somewhat restraining themselves.

I suppose they're worried that if Evan hears they've been bullying me, he might do something about it.

And they're confident I won't directly tell him that they're bullying me.

They're absolute geniuses.

If I just cut my arms a few times or tore off some skin, I could swallow my pride and tell someone.

But trapped in that room, dying so preciously, I can't bring myself to do something as pride-wounding as tattling.

"Evan found a nice restaurant recently."

"Why don't you two go together? It's a bit awkward with me tagging along."

"Then what would you eat, Miss?"

"I'll find somewhere that serves free lunch, I guess."

At those words, Vivian bites her lip slightly and forcibly grabs my arm, dragging me along.

"Ouch, let go of my arm."

"Oh, I'm sorry."

Vivian releases my arm at those words.

Instead, she takes my hand and continues pulling me along.

"Do you really have to take me with you?"

"Yes, even in places like that, there might be ill-mannered people who approach you. Miss, there are many people in this world who dislike nobles on principle. And if they find a noble they can harass, they certainly will."

"...I suppose so."

I wasn't convinced, but I closed my eyes and answered, knowing Vivian wouldn't let me go unless I responded.

At this point, I'm basically just an attachment doll.

Taking me everywhere, not respecting me as a human being with my own personality, ignoring my pleas not to touch me, dismissing my curses as nonsense even when I tell her I hate her, using spells to bind me when I try to escape, sticking to me even during the time between classes so I can't run away, not letting me choose my own meals, preventing me from taking my own life, taking away my only weapon—the pistol—and not returning it, not sending me back to my room, just keeping me trapped here for reasons I can't understand, why can't they just send me back?

"Erica. Miss Erica!"

"Ah, um. What?"

"We're here!"

Entering the restaurant, Evan was waiting at a table.

He gave me an awkward smile and said he had already ordered the food.

Seeing one dish and three meals, perhaps Evan was the one who asked for me to be brought along.

A server brought Evan a drink with unknown fruits floating in it, Vivian a tea whose aroma reached even here, and me a coffee filled with ice.

We began our meal.

The dish was simply a mixture of mushrooms and meat, and the meal was an exotic fried noodle dish.

"Hey, Vivian. Can you bring me some spices?"
data.epi_content2:
Vivian nodded and brought me the spices.

I don't know what the berries looked like, but it seemed like a mixture of pepper and rosemary.

I took the container and sprinkled it over my noodles.

I continued until I wondered if I might empty the entire container.

The aroma was so strong that Evan and Vivian frowned.

I ignored them and kept sprinkling.

When the red powder had almost completely covered the noodles, I handed the container back to Vivian to return to its place.

Taking a bite of the noodles, they were slightly spicy.

Maybe that's why it felt good.

Unless I cover it with spices, my sense of taste seems dulled and I can't taste much.

The two looked at me with sour expressions, but I didn't really care.

I wasn't going to touch the main dish anyway, so they could share it.

The meal ended in a gloomy atmosphere, and I continued to be dragged along on their date.

Eating dessert at a cafe, walking through the Academy gardens, watching them make jokes and subtly press against each other.

Feeling tortured by watching them, I sat on a bench by a pond where fish were swimming and said lightly:

"How long do I have to keep doing this?"

See, it feels like I've ruined the mood again.

Maybe it's just my own inferiority complex.

"No matter how much you laugh, talk, and buy me delicious food, I'm not enjoying any of this. It's actually painful. I hate seeing you two laugh in front of me, I hate the slight romantic atmosphere between you. I hate myself for thinking this way, and I hate sitting on the bed all day locked in Vivian's room."

They both fell silent.

Yes, I guess they don't want me to die.

Then at least create a better environment for me.

What do you want from me, keeping me trapped in a place like this?

"It's painful."

I get up and walk toward my room.

But of course that won't work.

Vivian followed me.

"Erica, let's go to my room."

"No."

"But..."

"But nothing, damn it, just leave me alone!!

Whether I live or die, can't you just leave me be and I'll manage on my own!? Why are you so desperate to keep me captive?

Are you afraid I'll hang myself like your father?"

Vivian's pupils dilated.

"That man was just kicked out of the organization because he had no ability but kept saying the right things!

Even in that circus of horse riders run by Evan's crazy family, people who can feed themselves get kicked out because a mere commoner dares to swing a sword and speak the truth!!

Why, do you see me overlapping with that man who was hanging there?"

Vivian trembled and began to cry slightly.

Rather than sobbing, it seemed like a mixture of anger and bewilderment, just tears flowing.

"I'm actually envious. That idiot just goes to heaven when he dies, but I'm trapped in this fucking place!!!"

I punched a nearby tree like a madwoman.

The skin on my hands peeled off and blood flowed, but the tree only swayed gently.

Feeling like even the tree was mocking my powerlessness, I punched it even harder.

When I clenched my fist, some unnamed bone protruded.

But rather than pain, I just felt a sting.

Yes, I just want to die and be done with it.

I'm trapped in the frame of time and can't escape at all.
data.epi_content3:
It's hell. It's hell. It's hell.

"Erica, don't say anything more..."

"Evan, do you think this is normal?

Or has your head finally gone crazy like your idiotic family?

Why, damn it, why are you so desperate to keep me locked up?! Why!!!"

Evan gritted his teeth and glared at me.

"It's all meaningless nonsense.

It has no meaning at all, no meaning!"

As if I were an actor on a stage, I stretched my arms in an exaggerated gesture toward the moon, bowed in reverence, and said with a smile:

"We all know what's happening to each other, why we're doing these things, why we can't solve problems even when they exist.

This is just, just, consuming emotions to death. Like searching for useless roots and honor.

Yes, like your family. Horse-riding circus performers, neigh-heehee! Ehihi."

I made a horse shape with my fingers, mimicked running, made horse sounds, and laughed.

"What on earth..."

The same question again.

"What on earth is wrong with me, you ask?

You know very well what the problem is.

It's just, just. I'm the problem.

I'm exhausted. Don't worry, this won't become an issue between families.

It's all over. It's all over, there's nothing left."

My head throbbed violently.

So I banged my forehead against the tree like a woodpecker.

Only then did I feel a little better.

"I have nothing left, and you just need to hold hands with Vivian and spend happy times together.

You still have so much.

So just leave me alo—damn it..."

Evan used a sleep spell.

It must have been that, given how desperately sleepy I felt.

Evan approached and embraced me as I was about to collapse.

I felt an emotion so disgusting it drove me mad.

I felt despair at the fact that I was moved and still liked Evan.

I looked into their eyes.

Vivian's and Evan's eyes.

Abnormal, sympathetic, pitiful, looking at me as something to be protected.

Despite deliberately saying sharp words to make them abandon me.

That's why it's utterly disgusting.

It's hypocrisy. A terrible kind of goodness that doesn't help the recipient at all.

A terrible kind of goodness where the giver feels satisfied, but the person receiving the kindness suffers even more.

I feel like I'm suffocating to death.

Before collapsing, I grabbed my throat tightly.

If I had a fork, I would have stabbed my neck.

I need a damn blade.

I need a blade to lightly scrape off skin.

I need a blade to drain this lady's blood and remove that precious self-esteem along with chunks of flesh.

Other people are hell.

The person who said this also said that human destiny is in human hands.

"This is... bullshit."

Then I guess I'm not human.

Even a drug addict locked in a mental hospital who calls himself unqualified to be human seems to be in a much better position than me.

Of course I'm livestock.

Unable to even choose the day I die, locked up somewhere with just a lifeline—if this isn't being livestock, what is it?
